title: Omicron Wave Heads for U.K., but It’s Not Clear How Bad It’ll Be
content: Britain could be a bellwether of what other countries will see from the new coronavirus variant. Officials say Omicron could account for most cases within weeks.
en_title: Omicron Wave Heads for U.K., but It’s Not Clear How Bad It’ll Be
en_content: Britain could be a bellwether of what other countries will see from the new coronavirus variant. Officials say Omicron could account for most cases within weeks.

title: Mexico Migrant Truck Crash Leaves 53 Dead
content: Dozens more were reported injured in the crash in southern Chiapas state, where many migrants regularly cross into Mexico from Central America.
en_title: Mexico Migrant Truck Crash Leaves 53 Dead
en_content: Dozens more were reported injured in the crash in southern Chiapas state, where many migrants regularly cross into Mexico from Central America.

title: U.K. Court Rules Julian Assange Can Be Extradited to U.S.
content: The WikiLeaks founder can still appeal the verdict, which would leave him facing espionage charges that could put him in prison for decades.
en_title: U.K. Court Rules Julian Assange Can Be Extradited to U.S.
en_content: The WikiLeaks founder can still appeal the verdict, which would leave him facing espionage charges that could put him in prison for decades.

title: Taiwan Loses Nicaragua as Ally as Tensions With China Rise
content: The nation switched diplomatic allegiance to Beijing, leaving 13 nations and the Vatican still recognizing Taiwan as a sovereign nation.
en_title: Taiwan Loses Nicaragua as Ally as Tensions With China Rise
en_content: The nation switched diplomatic allegiance to Beijing, leaving 13 nations and the Vatican still recognizing Taiwan as a sovereign nation.

title: As Muratov Accepts Nobel, Legacy of His Russian Predecessors Recedes
content: Dmitri A. Muratov is only the third Russian to win the Nobel Peace Prize, after Andrei D. Sakharov and Mikhail S. Gorbachev. The openness they championed is under assault.

des_facet: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

en_title: As Muratov Accepts Nobel, Legacy of His Russian Predecessors Recedes
en_content: Dmitri A. Muratov is only the third Russian to win the Nobel Peace Prize, after Andrei D. Sakharov and Mikhail S. Gorbachev. The openness they championed is under assault.

title: Turkey’s President Follows His Own Advice Even as Economy Slips
content: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is increasingly isolated as he clings to an economic prescription that few endorse but that he believes will bear results before elections.
en_title: Turkey’s President Follows His Own Advice Even as Economy Slips
en_content: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is increasingly isolated as he clings to an economic prescription that few endorse but that he believes will bear results before elections.

title: India’s Farmers Call Off Yearlong Protest Against Hated Farm Laws
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi unexpectedly conceded protesters’ main demand weeks ago, but serious problems remain with the country’s agricultural system.
en_title: India’s Farmers Call Off Yearlong Protest Against Hated Farm Laws
en_content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi unexpectedly conceded protesters’ main demand weeks ago, but serious problems remain with the country’s agricultural system.

title: Earth Is Getting a ‘Black Box’ to Hold Humans Accountable for Climate Change
content: When a plane crashes, its flight recorder is critical to piecing together the missteps that led to calamity. Now the planet is getting its own in case it self-destructs.
en_title: Earth Is Getting a ‘Black Box’ to Hold Humans Accountable for Climate Change
en_content: When a plane crashes, its flight recorder is critical to piecing together the missteps that led to calamity. Now the planet is getting its own in case it self-destructs.

title: Danish Leader Is Questioned Over ‘Minkgate’ Cull Driven by Covid Fears
content: The prime minister, Mette Frederiksen, said she did not know the government lacked legal authority to order the mass slaughter of 17 million minks after infected animals passed the virus to humans.

more_info.byline: By Thomas Erdbrink and Jasmina Nielsen

en_title: Danish Leader Is Questioned Over ‘Minkgate’ Cull Driven by Covid Fears
en_content: The prime minister, Mette Frederiksen, said she did not know the government lacked legal authority to order the mass slaughter of 17 million minks after infected animals passed the virus to humans.

title: Covid Malaise
content: Why do Americans say the economy is in rough shape? Because it is.

title: Rain-Soaked Rural Australia Asks: When Will It End?
content: After years of climate extremes, even the toughest, most community-minded Australians are desperate for a break from the deluge of disaster.
en_title: Rain-Soaked Rural Australia Asks: When Will It End?
en_content: After years of climate extremes, even the toughest, most community-minded Australians are desperate for a break from the deluge of disaster.

title: A New Ambition for the European Union: ‘Power’
content: President Emmanuel Macron of France laid out plans for a Europe that is “master of its own destiny.”
en_title: A New Ambition for the European Union: ‘Power’

content: The W.H.O. says the rollout leaves poor nations behind.

title: U.S. Announces End to Combat Mission in Iraq, but Troops Will Not Leave
content: The U.S. military said it had transitioned to an advise and assist mission in the country, but the roughly 2,500 service members on the ground will remain, staying on in support roles.

des_facet: United States Defense and Military Forces
org_facet: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

en_title: U.S. Announces End to Combat Mission in Iraq, but Troops Will Not Leave

title: Boris Johnson and Wife, Carrie Johnson, Announce Birth of Second Child
content: The birth comes as the British prime minister faces a political scandal over Covid rules.
en_title: Boris Johnson and Wife, Carrie Johnson, Announce Birth of Second Child
en_content: The birth comes as the British prime minister faces a political scandal over Covid rules.

title: What Data Shows About Vaccine Supply and Demand in the Most Vulnerable Places
content: Among the least vaccinated countries, some have used nearly all of the Covid vaccine doses available to them, while others have been able to distribute just a fraction of their available doses.

more_info.byline: By Keith Collins and Josh Holder

en_title: What Data Shows About Vaccine Supply and Demand in the Most Vulnerable Places
en_content: Among the least vaccinated countries, some have used nearly all of the Covid vaccine doses available to them, while others have been able to distribute just a fraction of their available doses.

title: The prime minister of Finland apologizes for going to clubs maskless after contact with an infected official.
content: Prime Minister Sanna Marin said she should have “double-checked the guidance” after someone in her government tested positive for the coronavirus.
en_title: The prime minister of Finland apologizes for going to clubs maskless after contact with an infected official.
en_content: Prime Minister Sanna Marin said she should have “double-checked the guidance” after someone in her government tested positive for the coronavirus.

title: New Zealand Plans to Eventually Ban All Cigarette Sales
content: The proposal, expected to become law next year, would raise the smoking age year by year until it covers the entire population.
en_title: New Zealand Plans to Eventually Ban All Cigarette Sales
en_content: The proposal, expected to become law next year, would raise the smoking age year by year until it covers the entire population.

title: Progress for Saudi Women Is Uneven, Despite Cultural Changes and More Jobs
content: Women say Saudi Arabia has advanced significantly in just the past year, with more choices regarding work, fashion (including colored abayas) and social spaces, but restrictions remain everywhere.

title: Ukraine Commanders Say a Russian Invasion Would Overwhelm Them
content: If Russia opts for an invasion, Ukraine’s generals say, they would have no hope of repelling it without a major infusion of military help from the West.
en_title: Ukraine Commanders Say a Russian Invasion Would Overwhelm Them
en_content: If Russia opts for an invasion, Ukraine’s generals say, they would have no hope of repelling it without a major infusion of military help from the West.

title: Biden Rallies Global Democracies as U.S. Hits a ‘Rough Patch’

title: Where the Despairing Log On, and Learn Ways to Die
content: It has the trappings of popular social media, a young audience and explicit content on suicide that other sites don’t allow. It is linked to a long line of lives cut short.

title: Indonesian Militant Sentenced to Life in Prison Over 2005 Bombing
content: A court said Upik Lawanga made bombs for an attack that killed 22 people at a market. He is a member of a terrorist group that bombed churches, hotels and a Bali nightclub in the 2000s.

en_content: A court said Upik Lawanga made bombs for an attack that killed 22 people at a market. He is a member of a terrorist group that bombed churches, hotels and a Bali nightclub in the 2000s.

title: Jailed Journalists Reach Record High for Sixth Year in 2021
content: The Committee to Protect Journalists, a press freedom monitoring group, said 293 journalists were behind bars this year, more than a quarter of them in China.
en_title: Jailed Journalists Reach Record High for Sixth Year in 2021
en_content: The Committee to Protect Journalists, a press freedom monitoring group, said 293 journalists were behind bars this year, more than a quarter of them in China.
